summary: American Romantic author. Most commonly identified by 'Young Goodman Brown' (Faith, serpent-shaped staff, Goody Cloyse, witches' sabbath), The Scarlet Letter (Hester Prynne, Arthur Dimmesdale, Pearl, Roger Chillingworth), and The House of the Seven Gables (Pyncheon family). Herman Melville dedicated Moby-Dick to him and wrote 'Hawthorne and His Mosses.' Changed spelling of family name (added 'w') to distance from Salem ancestry.

works.name: Young Goodman Brown
works.description: Hawthorne's most frequently clued short story. The protagonist walks through the woods with a man whose staff is shaped like a serpent, meets Goody Cloyse and Deacon Gookin, and discovers his wife Faith at a witches' sabbath/satanic ceremony. Faith's pink ribbon falls from the sky. Ends with 'no hopeful verse' on his tombstone. Published in Mosses from an Old Manse. Melville called it 'as deep as Dante.'

clues.clue: Goodman Brown walks with man whose staff resembles serpent; meets Goody Cloyse, Deacon Gookin
examples: The protagonist meets a man with a serpent-shaped staff while walking through the woods before seeing Goody Cloyse
a man who finds Goody Cloyse and his wife Faith among the attendees of a satanic ritual

clues.clue: Wife Faith at witches' sabbath; pink ribbon falls from sky
examples: a character's husband screams her name, then her pink ribbon falls from the sky
sees his wife Faith at a witches' sabbath

clues.clue: 'No hopeful verse' on his tombstone
examples: ends by noting that 'no hopeful verse' is written on the title character's tombstone

clues.clue: Melville called it 'as deep as Dante' in 'Hawthorne and His Mosses'
examples: This author called 'Young Goodman Brown' as 'deep as Dante'

works.name: The Scarlet Letter
works.description: Hawthorne's most famous novel. Hester Prynne wears the title scarlet letter 'A' after her affair with Reverend Arthur Dimmesdale, who fathers the child Pearl. Roger Chillingworth torments Dimmesdale. Dimmesdale reveals a symbol seared on his chest during an Election Day sermon on a scaffold, then dies. Opens by noting all societies provide for a prison and a cemetery. Framing story: narrator finds Jonathan Pue's manuscript while working at the Salem Custom House.

clues.clue: Hester Prynne wears scarlet letter; affair with Arthur Dimmesdale; child Pearl; Roger Chillingworth torments Dimmesdale
examples: Pearl is ostracised on the basis that her mother wears the title accessory, a symbol of her affair with Arthur Dimmesdale
Hester Prynne: who sews the title object to her clothing

clues.clue: Dimmesdale's Election Day sermon on scaffold; reveals symbol on chest; dies
examples: an election day sermon atop a scaffold triggers the death of Roger Chillingworth

clues.clue: Custom House framing story: narrator finds Jonathan Pue's manuscript while surveyor of Salem
examples: the narrator claims to have found a manuscript by Jonathan Pue while working as chief executive officer, or surveyor, of this Salem institution

clues.clue: Opens noting societies provide for a prison and a cemetery
examples: one of his novels notes how all societies invariably provide for a prison and a cemetery

works.name: The House of the Seven Gables
works.description: A novel about the Pyncheon family curse. Judge Jaffrey Pyncheon chokes on his own blood in an oaken chair. Hepzibah opens a shop to support her brother Clifford. Holgrave hypnotizes Phoebe while telling of Matthew Maule. The missing deed is found behind a portrait. Hawthorne labels it a 'romance' rather than a novel in the preface.

clues.clue: Judge Jaffrey Pyncheon chokes on blood in oaken chair; Hepzibah supports brother Clifford
examples: a fictional villain who chokes on his own blood in an oaken chair
Hepzibah Pyncheon opens a store to support her brother Clifford

clues.clue: Holgrave hypnotizes Phoebe while telling of Matthew Maule's family
examples: Holgrave hypnotizes Phoebe while telling of a family who lives at a house built by Matthew Maule

clues.clue: Hawthorne calls it a 'romance' not burdened by 'minute fidelity'; missing deed behind portrait
examples: Hawthorne's preface labels The House of the Seven Gables a work in this genre that, unlike a novel, is not burdened by 'a very minute fidelity'

works.name: Rappaccini's Daughter
works.description: Giovanni discovers that Beatrice has been made poisonous by her botanist father Giacomo Rappaccini's garden in Padua. Giovanni sees a purple handprint on his wrist. Professor Baglioni offers an antidote that kills Beatrice. Features an anecdote about a beautiful woman sent to Alexander the Great as a gift.

clues.clue: Beatrice made poisonous by father's garden; Giovanni gives antidote that kills her; Baglioni berates
examples: Giovanni realizes that the title character, Beatrice, has become as poisonous as the plants
Professor Baglioni gives Giovanni a vase crafted by Cellini that contains a supposed antidote

clues.clue: Lisabetta shows secret entrance to garden in Padua; purple handprint on Giovanni's wrist
examples: Lisabetta shows the protagonist a secret entrance to a garden in Padua before he notices a purple handprint on his wrist

works.name: The Birthmark
works.description: Scientist Aylmer creates an elixir to remove a hand-shaped crimson birthmark from his wife Georgiana's cheek. He tests it on a geranium first. His assistant is Aminadab. The birthmark's disappearance is compared to 'the stain of the rainbow fading out of the sky.' Georgiana dies.

clues.clue: Aylmer creates elixir to remove hand-shaped birthmark from wife Georgiana; she dies
examples: Aminadab helps Aylmer create an elixir to remove the title hand-shaped feature from Georgiana
Aylmer treat his new wife Georgianna

clues.clue: Aylmer dreams of himself and assistant Aminadab botching the surgery
examples: One character created by this author dreams of himself and his assistant, Aminadab, botching a surgery

works.name: Other Works and Biographical
works.description: Additional works include 'My Kinsman, Major Molineux' (Robin sees relative tarred and feathered; man with face painted red and black), 'The Celestial Railroad' (dream-journey through Pilgrim's Progress with Mr. Smooth-it-away), 'Ethan Brand' (searches for Unpardonable Sin, burns himself in lime kiln), 'Dr. Heidegger's Experiment' (Fountain of Youth, Widow Wycherley), 'Feathertop' (Mother Rigby's living scarecrow), 'Wakefield' (man moves across street for 20 years), 'The May-Pole of Merry Mount' (Endicott cuts down maypole at Edgar and Edith's wedding), 'Roger Malvin's Burial' (Reuben accidentally shoots son Cyrus), The Blithedale Romance (Miles Coverdale, Brook Farm, Hollingsworth, Zenobia, Priscilla), Fanshawe (first novel, burned copies). Melville dedicated Moby-Dick to him; 'Hawthorne and His Mosses' review. Changed name (added 'w') to distance from Salem witch trial judge ancestor. Complained about 'damned mob of scribbling women.' Frederick Crews' Freudian reading in The Sins of the Fathers. Invited Rebecca Harding Davis to Concord. Wrote Life of Franklin Pierce.

clues.clue: Melville dedicated Moby-Dick to Hawthorne; 'Hawthorne and His Mosses' — 'dropped germinous seeds into my soul'
examples: This dedicatee of Moby-Dick
a writer who posed as 'a Virginian spending July in Vermont' to praise a story collection

examples: a man who spent eighteen years searching for the 'Unpardonable Sin' throws himself into a lime kiln

examples: a dream-journey through the terrain described in John Bunyan's Pilgrim's Progress

examples: Mother Rigby creates a living scarecrow that falls apart after it looks in the mirror

clues.clue: Changed name from Hathorne to Hawthorne (added 'w') to distance from Salem witch trial judge ancestor
examples: Hawthorne added a 'w' to his own name to distance himself from an ancestor with this job

clues.clue: Brook Farm fictionalized in The Blithedale Romance; Miles Coverdale, Zenobia, Hollingsworth, Priscilla
examples: Hawthorne's time at Brook Farm inspired his novel about Miles Coverdale

clues.clue: 'Dr. Heidegger's Experiment': Fountain of Youth; Widow Wycherley; three men fight
examples: Three men fight over Widow Wycherley after the title character gives them water from the Fountain of Youth

comprehensive_summary:
Nathaniel Hawthorne is one of the most canonical American authors in quizbowl. 'Young Goodman Brown' is his most heavily clued short story, appearing with remarkable frequency: Goodman Brown walks through the woods with a man whose staff resembles a serpent, encounters Goody Cloyse and Deacon Gookin, and finds his wife Faith at a witches' sabbath. Faith's pink ribbon falling from the sky and the 'no hopeful verse' tombstone ending are standard clues. The story appears in Mosses from an Old Manse, which Melville reviewed, calling 'Young Goodman Brown' 'as deep as Dante.'

The Scarlet Letter serves as the primary giveaway identifier through Hester Prynne, Arthur Dimmesdale, Pearl, and Roger Chillingworth. Dimmesdale's Election Day sermon on the scaffold, the Custom House framing story, and the opening note about societies providing prisons and cemeteries are all recurring clues. The House of the Seven Gables appears through the Pyncheon family curse, Judge Pyncheon choking on blood, and the romance/novel preface distinction.

'Rappaccini's Daughter' (Beatrice, poisonous garden, Professor Baglioni's fatal antidote) and 'The Birthmark' (Aylmer, Georgiana, Aminadab) are both frequently clued stories about science gone wrong. Other commonly clued works include 'My Kinsman, Major Molineux' (tarring-and-feathering), 'Ethan Brand' (Unpardonable Sin, lime kiln), 'The Celestial Railroad' (Mr. Smooth-it-away), 'Feathertop' (Mother Rigby's scarecrow), and 'Dr. Heidegger's Experiment' (Fountain of Youth). The Blithedale Romance fictionalizes Brook Farm.

Biographically, Hawthorne is defined by his relationship with Melville (Moby-Dick dedication, 'Hawthorne and His Mosses'), his Salem ancestry (adding 'w' to the family name), and his complaint about 'a damned mob of scribbling women.' He wrote the campaign biography Life of Franklin Pierce and invited Rebecca Harding Davis to Concord after reading 'Life in the Iron Mills.'
